Heading Level Skip Test Page

h1 present above

Section A — skips h2

h1 → h3 skips one level (h2 missing)

This section jumps directly from h1 to h3, skipping h2 entirely. Screen readers and assistive technologies rely on a logical heading hierarchy to understand document structure. Skipping levels breaks that structure.

Subsection A.1 — skips h4
h3 → h5 skips one level (h4 missing)

Another skip: from h3 directly to h5, bypassing h4.

Section B

A second h3 section. The hierarchy is still broken from the initial skip.
